title: pleopeltis alansmithii
intro: Pleopeltis alansmithii, also known as the Alan Smith's polypody, is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to Central and South America, specifically found in countries such as Mexico, Costa Rica, and Ecuador. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and has been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. Pleopeltis alansmithii can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves are also used in folk medicine to make poultices and compresses for topical application.
